data.title: MTY Food Group Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87, revenue of $284.79M beats by $91.89M
data.description:
• MTY Food Group press release (OTCPK:MTYFF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87.
  • Revenue of $284.79M (+2.2% Y/Y) beats by $91.89M.
  • Normalized adjusted EBITDA increased 1% to reach $60.2 million in the quarter, compared to $59.5 million in Q1-24. 
  • System sales for the quarter improved by 2.5% or $33.1 million to reach $1,364.8 million compared to $1,331.7 million in Q1-24 primarily due to favourable foreign exchange.
  • Cash flows provided by operating activities were $58.8 million compared to $54.2 million in Q1-24, an increase of $4.6 million
  • Quarterly dividend payment of $0.33 per share on May 15, 2025.
  • For 2025, management expects stability in normalized adjusted EBITDA margins across all three of its segments, though the Company may experience seasonal fluctuations in corporate store margins. We also expect capex to be lower than prior year, resulting in a positive impact to free cash flows. Looking further ahead, MTY remains optimistic about its ability to drive margin improvement through positive unit growth, enhanced efficiencies, and an ongoing reduction in the number of less profitable corporate stores.

data.title: i-80 Gold COO & President Matthew Gili to step down
data.description:
i-80 Gold (NYSE:IAUX) said that Matthew Gili, President & COO will be stepping down from his role, but will continue to work in his capacity until a successor is appointed and for a transitionary period thereafter.

Matthew Gili has served as the company's President & COO since its inception in 2021.

The company has commenced the search for a successor to the position.

data.title: BlackRock Non-GAAP EPS of $11.30 beats by $1.09, revenue of $5.28B misses by $30M
data.description:
• BlackRock press release (NYSE:BLK): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $11.30 beats by $1.09.
  • Revenue of $5.28B (+11.6% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • 12% increase in revenue year-over-year reflects positive impact of markets, organic base fee growth and fees related to the GIP Transaction, as well as higher technology services and subscription revenue, partially offset by lower performance fees
  • $84 billion of quarterly total net inflows, reflecting 3% annualized organic asset growth, led by a record first quarter for iShares ETFs alongside private markets and active net inflows
  • AUM of $11.6T up from AUM of $11.55T at the end of Q4, average AUM of $11.69T up from $11.5T at the end of Q4.

data.title: Greenland takeover: Trump team eyes swaying public opinion
data.description:
The Trump administration is working towards persuading Greenlanders on the U.S. potentially taking control of the semi-autonomous Danish territory, instead of the threat of force, The New York Times reported, citing sources briefed on the matter.

President Donald Trump, who believes Greenland needs to join the U.S. for "national, and even international security" previously refused to rule out using military action to pursue the acquisition. He even sai the U.S. will make Greenlanders rich.

A source told NYT that the National Security Council recently sent instructions to several federal departments on the matter, although full details are unclear. The plan reportedly includes a public relations effort aimed at convincing Greenlanders that they should ask to join the U.S., which could include social media campaigns.

Trump advisers have explored financial incentives, including a potential annual payment of ~$10,000 per Greenlander to replace the $600M in subsidies that Denmark gives the island. The related costs could be offset by new revenue from Greenland's natural resources, which include rare earth minerals and oil. 

Climate change has been thawing Greenland's resource-rich areas, making them more commercially viable and opening up new sea routes for commercial shipping. But the U.S. has warned of the threat of encroachment by China and Russia.

Trump officials are claiming that only the U.S. can protect Greenland from such encroachment. Trump recently shared a video on how the U.S. stepped in to prevent the Nazis from potentially invading Greenland during World War II.

data.title: Brokerage flags downside risks to coffee prices associated with US tariffs
data.description:
Brokerage BMI on Friday made an upward revision to its 2025 price forecast for ICE-listed second-month arabica coffee (coffee C) futures, from USc240/lb to USc340/lb, as it expects a decline in production in Vietnam, and forecasts a lower harvest in Brazil.

The brokerage forecasts global production in 2024/25 to increase to 174.4mn bags from 168.1mn bags in 2023/24. 

However, on the demand side, it highlights the downside risks to coffee prices associatedwith the consequences of US tariffs on global growth. On Friday, prices (KC1:COM) were set for a third week of decline. 

Coffee prices (KC1:COM) experienced significant upward pressure over the first few months of 2025, reaching an all-time high of USc425.1/lb on February 13, 2025.

Between October 2024 and February 2025, prices increased for four consecutive months, with the price rally mostly being driven by supply-side concerns relating to Brazil and Vietnam, two major exporters in the market. 

Nonetheless, prices have experienced some volatility in April thus far, as market sentiment first turned negative following the announcement of US reciprocal tariffs on coffee-exporting countries on April 2, before recovering slightly after the 90-day pause was announced on April 9, BMI Research said.

“With worries about the potential effects and timeline of U.S. tariffs, we have also seen an increase in volatility in the market,” BMI said, adding, intra-day volatility for coffee prices was 6.2% and 7.4%, respectively on April 4 and April 7.

The US essentially relies entirely on coffee imports to meet domestic needs, so the imposition of tariffs on imports of this commodity has the potential to significantly influence demand, the brokerage concluded.

data.title: Constellation Brands targets $9B in operating cash flow through FY28 amid beer demand challenges
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Constellation Brands (STZ) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Bill Newlands highlighted that Constellation Brands achieved enterprise net sales growth and double-digit comparable EPS growth in fiscal 2025 despite a challenging macroeconomic environment. He emphasized the company's focus on driving distribution gains, disciplined innovation, and increased marketing investments to support its Beer Business.
  • The anticipated closing of the 2025 Wine Divestitures Transaction is expected to improve performance in the Wine and Spirits Business, with associated restructuring actions projected to yield over $200M in net annualized cost savings by fiscal 2028.
  • CFO Garth Hankinson noted the company’s three-year $4B share repurchase authorization, reflecting confidence in the company’s valuation and future performance.

Outlook

  • Beer net sales growth is projected between 0% to 3% for fiscal 2026, with an operating income growth range of 0% to 2%.
  • Operating margins for fiscal 2027-2028 are expected to stabilize at 39% to 40%.
  • The company is targeting $9B in operating cash flow and $6B in free cash flow from FY26 to FY28, driven by investments in brewery expansions and operational efficiencies.

Financial Results

  • Constellation Brands confirmed it achieved double-digit comparable EPS growth in fiscal 2025, supported by operating margin improvements.
  • The Beer Business maintained its industry-leading performance, with significant focus on Hispanic consumer loyalty and distribution gains.
  • Wine and Spirits restructuring is anticipated to contribute $100M in cost savings by FY26, with the full $200M realized by FY28.

Q&A

  • Lauren Lieberman, Barclays, inquired about tariff impacts on beer and aluminum cans. CEO Newlands confirmed compliance with USMCA standards and noted that all announced tariffs are incorporated into guidance.
  • Nik Modi, RBC Capital Markets, asked about the significant reduction in beer guidance. CEO Newlands attributed this to near-term socioeconomic challenges, emphasizing strong brand health and long-term growth potential.
  • Bonnie Herzog, Goldman Sachs, sought clarity on beer margins amid volume slowdowns. CFO Hankinson outlined strategies including cost savings, incremental volume growth, and pricing initiatives to maintain margins at 39% to 40%.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern over the revised beer growth projections and the impact of tariffs, with questions focusing on macroeconomic volatility and consumer spending patterns.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, emphasizing brand health and strategic investments to ensure long-term growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management demonstrated a heightened focus on external economic challenges and their implications for near-term performance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • A significant reduction in medium-term beer growth guidance, now targeting 2% to 4% for fiscal 2027-2028, compared to prior targets of 7% to 9%.
  • Increased emphasis on cost savings and operational efficiencies as part of the restructuring plan for the Wine and Spirits Business.
  • Analysts displayed increased skepticism compared to the previous quarter, particularly regarding macroeconomic assumptions and their impact on guidance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified socioeconomic factors, including inflation and consumer spending shifts, as key risks to near-term performance.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of tariffs on aluminum and other materials, as well as the broader economic outlook for key consumer demographics.
  • Mitigation strategies include disciplined cost management, modular expansion of brewery capacity, and targeted marketing investments.

Final Takeaway

Constellation Brands remains focused on navigating a challenging economic environment through strategic investments in its Beer Business and restructuring of its Wine and Spirits portfolio. While the company has adjusted its medium-term growth outlook, management emphasized robust brand health and operational efficiencies as key drivers for future performance. The $4B share repurchase authorization and $9B operating cash flow target for FY26-FY28 further underscore confidence in the company’s long-term strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Carmax highlights 67% omni-channel retail penetration in Q4 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CarMax (KMX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Bill Nash emphasized the company's strong execution across its diversified business, leading to increased unit volumes in retail and wholesale sales, improved gross profit, and a record high dealer vehicle acquisition. He highlighted that omni-channel capabilities enabled 67% of retail unit sales to be omnichannel, up from 64% in the previous year.
  • CFO Enrique Mayor-Mora reported Q4 diluted EPS of $0.58, an 81% increase year-over-year. Adjusted EPS was $0.64, excluding a $12 million non-cash impairment related to an Edmunds lease. Total gross profit reached $668 million, up 14% year-over-year, with contributions from retail margin growth and efficiency improvements.
  • Jon Daniels, Senior Vice President of CarMax Auto Finance (CAF), noted that CAF increased its income by $12 million to $159 million for the quarter, driven by a steady net interest margin of 6.2% and controlled loan losses.

Outlook

  • Management emphasized their focus on growing market share, retail and wholesale sales, and achieving double-digit EPS growth. They plan to leverage data science and AI to enhance digital experiences and efficiency.
  • For fiscal 2026, the company anticipates six new store openings and four standalone reconditioning and auction centers, alongside $575 million in capital expenditures.
  • CFO Mayor-Mora outlined plans for a slight year-over-year increase in extended protection plan (EPP) margins and service margin growth, with service expected to contribute positive profit for the full year.

Financial Results

  • Q4 total sales were $6 billion, reflecting a 7% increase driven by higher volumes. Retail unit sales grew 6.2%, while used unit comps rose 5.1%. Wholesale unit sales increased by 3.1%.
  • Retail gross profit per used unit was $2,322, a record for the fourth quarter and up from $2,251 the previous year. Wholesale gross profit per unit was $1,045, slightly lower than $1,120 a year ago.
  • Service gross profit saw a $44 million improvement over the prior year, driven by cost efficiencies and growth in sales.
  • The company repurchased approximately 1.2 million shares for $99 million during the quarter, leaving $1.94 billion in remaining repurchase authorization.

Q&A

  • Sharon Zackfia, William Blair: Asked about sales momentum differences between the first and second halves of fiscal 2025. CEO Nash attributed gains to better execution, inventory acquisition expansion, and a more stable pricing environment.
  • Seth Basham, Wedbush Securities: Inquired about the impact of potential new car tariffs. CEO Nash stated that higher new car prices could widen the price gap, potentially increasing demand for late-model used cars, though rising parts costs may challenge reconditioning expenses.
  • John Murphy, Bank of America: Asked about capacity for six to 10-year-old vehicles and cost savings. CEO Nash confirmed that new facilities would increase capacity and that logistics improvements would reduce costs.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on competitive positioning and macroeconomic factors like tariffs and pricing trends.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during the presentation, emphasizing strategic progress and operational efficiency gains. CEO Nash presented a forward-looking perspective on initiatives, such as leveraging omni-channel capabilities and AI-driven tools.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts showed increased confidence, reflecting strengthened financial performance and operational improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Retail and wholesale unit sales growth accelerated compared to Q3, supported by improved pricing stability and operational efficiencies.
  • Omni-channel retail penetration increased to 67% in Q4, up from 64% in Q3.
  • Management's tone remained consistently forward-looking, with a stronger emphasis on leveraging technological advancements and efficiency gains.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential risks from new car tariffs, which could impact parts costs and pricing dynamics.
  • Analysts raised concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties and their potential influence on consumer demand and credit performance.
  • Strategies to mitigate risks include ongoing cost control measures, enhanced inventory sourcing, and leveraging AI for operational efficiencies.

Final Takeaway

CarMax demonstrated robust Q4 2025 performance with record-high gross profit per retail unit and expanded omni-channel penetration, underscoring its operational efficiency and strategic focus. Management's forward-looking initiatives, including leveraging AI and expanding capacity, position the company for continued growth despite macroeconomic uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: NVS, JOBY, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped slightly early Friday as investors weighed the intensifying trade conflict between the U.S. and China. Beijing escalated its response to President Donald Trump’s reciprocal tariffs by lifting its duties on U.S. imports to 125%, up from 84%. Meanwhile, the Trump administration confirmed that total U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods have now reached an effective rate of 145%.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Novartis (NYSE:NVS) +3% – Shares rose after the company announced its plans to invest a $23B to build and expand 10 U.S. facilities over five years, aiming to produce all key medicines domestically. The move comes amid tariff threats from President Trump on pharmaceutical imports. Plans include a new R&D center in San Diego and six new manufacturing sites, expected to create over 5,000 jobs. Novartis reaffirmed its full-year guidance and long-term growth targets, including a core margin of over 70% by 2027.

Biggest stock losers

  • Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) -3% – Shares extended losses after a 12% drop in the prior session, as Q1 2025 global shipments fell 9% Y/Y to 1.2M units, driven by production setbacks in North America and weaker volumes in Enlarged Europe. Despite this, the company saw U.S. sales growth for Jeep Compass, Grand Cherokee, and Ram trucks and improved EU30 market share to 17.3%. Shipments rose 4% in Stellantis’ “Third Engine” region, led by 19% growth in South America, while a 15% decline in the Middle East & Africa was tied to import restrictions.
  • Joby Aviation (NYSE:JOBY) -3% – Shares slipped after Morgan Stanley downgraded the stock to Equal-weight from Over-weight, citing rising tariff-related risks. Analyst Kristine Liwag noted that ongoing trade tensions could further disrupt an already fragile supply chain, prompting a shift toward more defensive aerospace names with stronger pricing power.

data.title: Alibaba’s Jack Ma says AI should support humans, not replace them - report
data.description:
Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) Co-Founder Jack Ma said that AI should not replace humans but work towards meeting their every need, Bloomberg News reported.

During a rare appearance on Thursday, the Chinese tech tycoon told employees that the technology should be made to serve people and make their lives better. It should protect livelihoods, the report added.

“We’re not trying to make machines more like humans. We’re trying to make them understand humans, to think like us and do things we can’t,” Ma said to employees at the company's office in Hangzhou, China, according to the report.

Ma noted that it is the responsibility of technologists to see that AI knows and supports humankind.

Alibaba did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Alibaba is among several Chinese companies that is trying to get a piece of the growing AI market. Earlier this week, the Chinese tech giant upgraded its AI services available abroad, aiming to reach more global customers. The company is offering its latest large language models, or LLMs, Qwen-Max, the reasoning model QwQ-Plus, the visual reasoning model QVQ-Max and the end-to-end multimodal model Qwen2.5-Omni-7b.

Globally, companies are advancing their research and investment amid the fierce AI race. Earlier this year, Chinese AI startup DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) rattled the tech sector after it unveiled its AI model R1 and detailed in a paper how large language models, or LLMs, could be built on tight budgets and improved without human supervision.

Alibaba plans to launch a new version of its AI model Qwen 3 later this month. The Chinese c-commerce giant had released the Qwen 2.5 Qwen 2.5-VL family of its AI models in January.

In February, Alibaba's CEO Eddie Wu said the company’s “primary objective” was now artificial general intelligence, or AGI.

AGI refers to the hypothetical intelligence of a machine that has the ability to understand or learn any intellectual task that a human can. It is a type of AI that aims to mimic the cognitive abilities of the human brain.

data.title: Stock index futures slide, yields mixed as China slaps 125% tariffs on U.S. goods
data.description:
Stock index futures slid on Friday as the U.S.-China trade war seemed to heat up as China further retaliated against President Donald Trump's tariffs.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.7%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 3.81%.

China has announced an increase in tariffs on U.S. goods to 125%, up from the previous rate of 84%, effective April 12, and stated it will disregard any additional tariff hikes announced by Washington moving forward.

Wall Street's euphoria disappeared on Thursday as market participants were back in selloff mode.

"The main driver of the renewed market pressure was an increased focus on the US-China escalation, with yesterday’s market downturn accelerating after a White House clarification that total tariffs on China would now be 145% rather than 125%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

While this difference is negligible in any practical economic sense, the market reaction showed an increased sensitivity to the risks of a disorderly economic decoupling between the world’s two largest economies that we highlighted yesterday, Sidorov added.

Trump’s administration clarified the 125% tax on imports from China was 145%. This increase will not shift demand patterns. For investors, this raises policy competence questions again. A well-planned trade tax would consider costs. Tax rates set seemingly at random question whether costs are properly considered, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent said that they saw nothing unusual in yesterday’s market moves. The 30-year Treasury yield experienced the biggest increase since 1982, equities are falling, the dollar is falling, and gold hit an all-time high. Economists might consider this pattern not entirely usual," Donovan added. 

On the economic side, the March wholesale inflation data will land before the bell. Core PPI is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M and 3.6% Y/Y.

PPI is expected to come in at 3.3% Y/Y and 0.2% M/M.    

The March consumer sentiment report will land later in the day and is forecasted to come in at 54.

Traders will also keep a close eye on the banks' Q1 earnings starting on Friday.
